topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my phone, a sudden, sharp intrusion into the quiet hum of my afternoon. It was a follow request. My stomach clenched, a tight, cold knot forming beneath my ribs. I stared at the screen, the profile picture—Professor Albright’s—staring back with an unnervingly familiar gaze. I hadn't interacted with him outside of mandatory lecture attendance, and now this. My fingers hovered over the screen, trembling slightly. A wave of confusion washed over me, a dizzying sense of having misplaced the map to my own routine. I checked my notifications again, half-expecting a reply, a clarification, anything to anchor me back to the predictable structure of my academic life. The fluorescent lights of the library seemed too bright, the silence too heavy.

[story 2]

I found myself pausing mid-sentence during the group project meeting, my gaze snagged by a profile picture I hadn't expected to see. It was Mr. Harrison, my notoriously strict history teacher, sporting a casual, smiling selfie. A strange flutter started in my chest, quickly escalating into a churning sensation. I excused myself abruptly, claiming a sudden need for water, and retreated to the relative solitude of the hallway. Every time I looked back at my phone, the world seemed to tilt slightly on its axis. Why was this happening? It felt like a misplaced piece in a carefully constructed puzzle, something fundamentally out of place. I ran a hand through my hair, the texture suddenly feeling alien against my fingertips.

[story 3]

The email arrived late, tucked between legitimate syllabus updates and mandatory reading lists. It was a simple message: "Following." My breath hitched. I reread the single word three times, the ink blurring slightly under my anxious scrutiny. I felt a strange detachment, as if I were watching a performance I hadn't auditioned for. My usual focus—the immediate task at hand—evaporated. I paced the small confines of my dorm room, the floorboards seeming to shift beneath my bare feet. A hollow feeling settled in my chest, a vacuum where certainty used to reside. I kept checking my social media feed, a frantic, repetitive motion, searching for context, for an explanation that didn't exist.
